yellow rat genocide clear!!!! that whole thing was a blast lol, final boss was super fun
i loved how laid back it was, it was really satisfying slowly getting stronger and just obliterating everything and getting 50 billion coins
i think the very first pepper felt a little meh? but thats the extent of what i remember, everything else just feels kinda "yeah"
at some point it kinda doesnt matter what specifically they do? it all just gets sucked into the whirlwind of chaos
no just the upgrades in general, like there were times when i didnt even notice what a specific pepper was doing and it was just like "whoa lightshow"
i wouldnt necessarily say thats a flaw actually
go in the warp its awesome probably
i think its fine to just have everything feed into a chaotic power trip
the only thing is like, the bosses sometimes felt like an odd juxtaposition with how chill and zen the actual gameplay is?
like 90% of the game is just satisfying blowing things up and you dont really have to actively try, and then suddenly the bosses show up and theres actual difficulty
i think it works fine for who the recipient is, alice loves herself some good bosses
but just as a general design thing there is a bit of a disconnect
maybe it would have been cool to hook the incremental mechanics into the bosses somehow? like, kinda like a raid boss
yeah and its like, maybe you dont necessarily "die" and you CAN just grind the bosses as big ol' damage sponges, but you can beat them considerably faster if you actually play well
gg
you could even throw in the time limit again so its like a balance between good upgrades and actual skill
or maybe the upgrades are used by the bosses instead of you so if you bought a lot of them you are fucked
are you having fun and smiling today?
the first way to atone is to apologize to those you've wronged
